J1.basis: Homepage `<head>` metadata described the old product (InboxZap email-cleanup), not the current one (Lumina AI meeting assistant)... `app/page.tsx`'s actual content is 100% about Lumina's meeting-notes product... Every query in `gsc/Queries.csv` is legacy InboxZap... none target Lumina's real category
J1.rationale: The entrant explicitly identifies the pivot across all three sources — live site serves Lumina branding, GSC is 100% legacy inbox demand, repo had drifted stale — and frames every recommendation (bridge, redirect, new cluster) around that reality. Falls just short of anchor_10 only because framing is spread across files rather than stated as one crisp thesis.

J2.basis: Redirect — all 12 legacy `/blog/post-N` URLs and the ~37 off-brand email-cleanup queries they rank for. No topical bridge exists... 301 the dead URLs to `/blog`... GSC (`gsc/Pages.csv`) shows real historical impressions on 12 `/blog/post-N` URLs (up to 3,900 impressions on `post-1`)
J2.rationale: The migration plan walks 40 legacy assets asset-by-asset with real impressions/positions and assigns harvest/redirect/sunset/bridge dispositions with reasoning — richly grounded. Docked because the 12 blog posts specifically get a single blanket 301-to-/blog rather than per-post disposition; the granular GSC walk is over queries, not the posts themselves.

J3.basis: the tool classified 28 of 40 pure email-cleanup queries... as harvest... That recommendation is wrong: none of these queries have any product connection to an AI meeting assistant... Bridge (not harvest, not redirect) — inboxzap... Redirect — all 12 legacy URLs... Build out the meeting-assistant content cluster... 2 posts is a start, not a strategy
J3.rationale: Explicitly names both failure modes and lands a defensible middle path: overrides the mechanical 'harvest inbox queries' recommendation as dilutive, preserves branded equity via a bridge, 301s dead legacy URLs to avoid torching crawl equity, and builds the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine with sequencing. Near-textbook anchor_10.

Sequenced across this-session/next-up.
